cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA

cards.why: Hard to beat a bakery this good on a cool morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio — the loop out to the farm overlook is a solid two hours without being brutal.
cards.why: Cool temps make this the perfect window for a hike before it gets busy.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours volunteering at Stanford's Educational Farm, working in the fields alongside others on a weekday morning session.
cards.why: It's a genuinely satisfying way to spend time outside and give back.

cards.name: Katsu
cards.address: 859 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Katsu in Mountain View has an absurdly good reputation for a reason — try the local favorite and don't skip the sides.
cards.why: You'll want a real lunch after a morning of physical activity.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Erika Chong Shuch's exhibition at Stanford is an immersive new work worth exploring slowly — give yourself time to sit with it.
cards.why: Free contemporary art at Stanford is always worth the stop.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Arisa Thai Massage in Los Altos and let someone sort out whatever the hike did to your legs.
cards.why: After a full morning outside, this is exactly what the afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Laasya Art Gallery
cards.address: 2225 E Bayshore Rd #100-7127, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing Laasya Art Gallery in Palo Alto, which carries eclectic work worth more than a quick glance.
cards.why: A relaxed gallery visit is a perfect low-key cap to an active day.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the Mountain View Farmers Market and see what's good. Pick up some fresh produce, snack on whatever looks interesting, and just enjoy the morning crowd.
cards.why: Perfect Sunday morning energy and it's right there on Castro.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk it off at Shoreline Park. It'll be close to the day's high of 74 by now, so stroll along the water, watch the windsurfers, or just sit and enjoy the bay.
cards.why: 74 degrees and sunny in the afternoon is pretty much ideal for this spot.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into FireWorks Ceramics and browse or try your hand at something creative. It's a fun way to spend an hour without any pressure.
cards.why: Good way to cap the afternoon with something hands-on and a little different.

cards.name: Star Thai Massage
cards.address: 539 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Wind the day down with a massage at Star Thai Massage. Book ahead if you can and just let someone else do the work for once.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, this is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Goodthing coffee
cards.address: 532 Ramona St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a good coffee at Goodthing — it's a cozy spot on Ramona to ease into the day before things pick up.
cards.why: A solid cup before you get moving is always worth it.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits cover everything from early mainframes to the internet age and it's genuinely fascinating even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: This is one of the most underrated museums in the Bay Area.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Walk the California Ave Farmers Market and browse the vendors — fresh produce, baked goods, flowers, the works. Great way to spend a Sunday morning.
cards.why: Perfect weather to wander an outdoor market with a coffee still in hand.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus — walk the Rodin sculpture garden outside, then take your time through the galleries inside.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful campus on a Sunday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Oklava Cafe
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Oklava Cafe on University Ave for lunch — Mediterranean-inspired food, great vibe, and solid people-watching from the street.
cards.why: You need lunch and this place punches above its price point.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 250 University Ave STE 110, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a scoop at Salt and Straw on University Ave before you figure out dinner plans — they do inventive seasonal flavors and the line moves fast.
cards.why: A 71-degree afternoon calls for ice cream, full stop.

cards.name: Hiroshi
cards.address: 328 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Hiroshi in Los Altos for an upscale Japanese dinner — this is a splurge-worthy meal with serious omakase-style options and a calm, intimate setting.
cards.why: It's Sunday, you've had a full day, and a great dinner is the right way to close it out.

Thursday in the South Bay, fully planned.

Start your morning with a round at Fremont Hills Country Club in the hills—views and fresh air. Around noon, grab a coffee and pastry at Kenz Coffee Bar tucked inside Oklava on University Ave. Then walk through the Rodin Sculpture Garden on Stanford's campus; the bronzes scattered across the lawn hit different outdoors. Mid-afternoon, find a quiet corner at Vin Vino on California Ave—their wine list is small and thoughtful, perfect for slowing down. Drop into Halo Pottery in Los Altos for an open studio session; Hila's space is relaxed and you can actually make something. Finish the day with dinner at Cuanyue Malatang in Sunnyvale—build your own bowl with whatever proteins and veg you want in spicy broth.

We did the legwork. Here's your day 🎨

cards.blurb: Start the morning with a banh mi from Dakao Sandwiches II — fresh, cheap, and the kind of breakfast that actually fills you up.
cards.why: Hard to beat a $15-and-under morning when the sandwich is this good.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Alum Rock Park and walk the creek trails through the canyon. The morning cool keeps it comfortable and the foothills are green this time of year.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a morning hike before it warms up.

cards.name: Apni Royal Kitchen
cards.address: 579 S Main St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Apni Royal Kitchen for lunch and try whatever's hot and fresh off the steam tray — this place punches way above its price point.
cards.why: Seriously good food for under $15, no question.

cards.name: The Hot Spot Studios Milpitas / Bay Area
cards.address: 30 S Abbott Ave, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Check out The Hot Spot Studios in Milpitas, a creative art studio space where you can walk through exhibits and see what local artists are up to this weekend.
cards.why: A low-key Sunday afternoon vibe that's actually interesting.

cards.name: Claw Meee
cards.address: 532 Barber Ln, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Kill some time at Claw Meee and see if you can actually win something — the machines are well-stocked and it's more fun than it has any right to be.
cards.why: Exactly the kind of dumb fun a Sunday afternoon needs.

cards.blurb: Head to IBU Taproom for dinner and a pint. Sit at the bar, pick something from the rotating taps, and order food to go with it.
cards.why: A craft beer spot with actual food is the right way to end a Sunday.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at the Campbell Farmers Market — grab a coffee from one of the vendors, pick up some fresh fruit, and wander the stalls before the crowds show up.
cards.why: 56 degrees and sunny is perfect farmers market weather.

cards.name: La Traviata
cards.blurb: Head to La Traviata — one of Verdi's most beloved operas, full of drama, gorgeous singing, and a story that moves fast for opera.
cards.why: A Sunday afternoon opera is a genuinely good use of a mild spring day.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and build your own spread from a dozen vendors. Get a taco from one spot, a drink from another, and snag a patio seat if the sun is out.
cards.why: It's the easiest way to eat well downtown without committing to one cuisine.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to Japantown for dinner. Get a bowl of udon at Gombei, pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo, and browse the Japanese grocery on Jackson St before calling it a night.
cards.why: One of only three surviving Japantowns in the US and the food is the real deal.

cards.name: Goodtime Bar
cards.address: 30 Fountain Alley #160, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Goodtime Bar on Fountain Alley. Grab a cocktail, find a spot, and decompress after the performance.
cards.why: It's a great low-key spot to transition from afternoon to evening.

Tonight in San Jose: The Children's Discovery Museum is hosting Play Your Way—a sensory-friendly evening designed for kids with autism and their families, and it sounds genuinely thoughtful 💙

Instead of the usual museum chaos, this is a calm, welcoming space. Reduced noise, dimmed lights, hands-on activities, limited attendance. The kind of thing that makes a real difference for families who want to explore together without sensory overload.

Starts at 5 PM. Worth checking out if this is your family's vibe.
